name: Peano Arithmetic & Paris — Part 11: Hydra Game
description: Hercules-Hydra game: Hydra is a rooted finite tree; Hercules chops a leaf; Hydra grows n copies of the subtree above the chopped node's parent (n = distance from root). Hercules always wins, but termination is unprovable in PA. Proof: assign ordinals to trees; each move strictly decreases ordinal; requires ε₀ induction.
mermaid:
graph TD
    DefHydra["Def: Hydra = rooted finite tree; heads = leaves"]
    DefMove["Def: Move: chop leaf; parent node grows n copies of subtree above it"]
    DefOrdTree["Def: Ordinal assignment: tree T ↦ ω^T₁ + ω^T₂ + ... (Cantor normal form)"]
    LemOrdDec["Lem: Each move strictly decreases tree ordinal"]
    LemWellFound["Lem: Ordinals well-founded ⇒ no infinite descending chain"]
    ThmHercules["Thm: Hercules always wins (any strategy)"]
    RefGoodstein["Ref: Goodstein / Indicators (Part 10)"]
    LemHydraEps0["Lem: Hydra termination equivalent to ε₀ well-founded"]
    LemEps0PA["Lem: PA ⊬ ε₀ well-founded"]
    ThmHydraUnprov["Thm: Kirby-Paris: Hydra termination unprovable in PA"]
    CorHydraProv["Cor: Provable in PA + TI(ε₀) or ACA₀"]
    DefHydra --> DefMove
    DefMove --> DefOrdTree
    DefOrdTree --> LemOrdDec
    LemOrdDec --> LemWellFound
    LemWellFound --> ThmHercules
    RefGoodstein --> LemHydraEps0
    ThmHercules --> LemHydraEps0
    LemHydraEps0 --> ThmHydraUnprov
    LemEps0PA --> ThmHydraUnprov
    ThmHydraUnprov --> CorHydraProv
    classDef definition fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef lemma fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff,stroke:#4dabf7
    classDef theorem fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    classDef corollary fill:#1abc9c,color:#fff,stroke:#16a085
    classDef ref fill:#bdc3c7,color:#333,stroke:#95a5a6
    class RefGoodstein ref
    class DefHydra,DefMove,DefOrdTree definition
    class LemOrdDec,LemWellFound,LemHydraEps0,LemEps0PA lemma
    class ThmHercules,ThmHydraUnprov theorem
    class CorHydraProv corollary
